dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S1572 RS: Federal Carjacking Enforcement Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mrs. Blackburn (for herself, Mr. Luján, Ms. Cortez Masto, Mr. Cornyn, Mr. Tillis, Ms. Klobuchar, Mr. Hagerty, Mrs. Shaheen, Mrs. Britt, Mr. Budd, Mr. Cruz, Mr. Lee, Mr. Hawley, Mr. Warner, Mr. Schmitt, Mrs. Moody, and Mr. Graham) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary
Reported by Mr. Grassley, with an amendment
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to improve the Federal carjacking statute.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Carjacking Enforcement Act.
Section 2119 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking , with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm and inserting knowingly; and
in paragraph (3), by striking if death results, and inserting if the motor vehicle is taken with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm, and death results,.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Federal Carjacking Enforcement Act.
Section 2119 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking , with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm and inserting knowingly;
in paragraph (1), by striking both, and inserting both;;
by striking paragraph (2) and inserting the following:
be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than 25 years, or both, if—

clause.text: the motor vehicle is taken with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm; or
the person taking the motor vehicle brandishes or discharges a firearm (as defined in section 921) during or in relation to such taking; and

subparagraph.text: serious bodily injury (as defined in section 1365, including any conduct that, if the conduct occurred in the special maritime and territorial jurisdiction of the United States, would violate section 2241 or 2242) results; and

paragraph.text: in paragraph (3), by striking if death results, and inserting if the motor vehicle is taken with the intent to cause death or serious bodily harm, and death results,.
